input.issue_title: Periods (punctuation) missing in "Repository contents" section in README.md
input.issue_body: e.g., right after "include/db.h: Main interface to the DB: Start here".

ground_truth.diff:
commit 9ee91ac747ddf26f484d54f9aa474ccc4a2e0359
Author: Chris Mumford <cmumford@google.com>
Date:   Wed Jun 12 15:28:22 2019 -0700

    Ending sentences with periods in README.md.
    
    This change was submitted in https://github.com/google/leveldb/pull/575
    by @prajwalchalla.
    
    This fixes issue #523.
    
    PiperOrigin-RevId: 252912613

diff --git a/README.md b/README.md
index 0b660ae..0a312ab 100644
--- a/README.md
+++ b/README.md
@@ -193,19 +193,19 @@ The public interface is in include/*.h.  Callers should not include or
 rely on the details of any other header files in this package.  Those
 internal APIs may be changed without warning.
 
 Guide to header files:
 
-* **include/db.h**: Main interface to the DB: Start here
+* **include/db.h**: Main interface to the DB: Start here.
 
 * **include/options.h**: Control over the behavior of an entire database,
 and also control over the behavior of individual reads and writes.
 
 * **include/comparator.h**: Abstraction for user-specified comparison function.
 If you want just bytewise comparison of keys, you can use the default
 comparator, but clients can write their own comparator implementations if they
-want custom ordering (e.g. to handle different character encodings, etc.)
+want custom ordering (e.g. to handle different character encodings, etc.).
 
 * **include/iterator.h**: Interface for iterating over data. You can get
 an iterator from a DB object.
 
 * **include/write_batch.h**: Interface for atomically applying multiple
@@ -217,9 +217,9 @@ length into some other byte array.
 * **include/status.h**: Status is returned from many of the public interfaces
 and is used to report success and various kinds of errors.
 
 * **include/env.h**:
 Abstraction of the OS environment.  A posix implementation of this interface is
-in util/env_posix.cc
+in util/env_posix.cc.
 
 * **include/table.h, include/table_builder.h**: Lower-level modules that most
-clients probably won't use directly
+clients probably won't use directly.

input.issue_body:
https://github.com/google/leveldb/blob/master/db/db_bench.cc#L190
Uninitialize variables:start_. See lin 190 for more information

input.issue_title: New max file size option is not available from C API
input.issue_body:
The C API misses a `leveldb_options_set_max_file_size` function.
Pull requests is coming up. 

BTW: The new max_file_size works great and gives us a big performance boost for multi-GB datasets.
